title: Add Party/Buyer GST & Address to Version 24 UI with Invoice Display

requirements.text: Restore the full Version 24 UI exactly as it was, including the company logo, layout, color scheme, navigation, and all existing components (summary cards, bill table, add bill form, search, party summary, company report, settings, invoice print view). Do not alter any existing Version 24 design or functionality.

quotes: Please copy whole UI of version 24
whole UI of version 24 and logo also

acceptanceCriteria: All Version 24 pages render identically to Version 24 (summary, add-bill, search, parties, company-report, settings, invoice print)
Company logo appears in the header/invoice exactly as in Version 24
No existing Version 24 UI elements are removed or repositioned

requirements.text: Add a party/buyer section to the backend Bill data model with three new fields: partyGstNumber (Text), partyAddress (Text). The party name is already stored; ensure partyGstNumber and partyAddress are persisted on each bill record. Update addBill and editBill backend functions to accept and store these fields. Ensure backward compatibility with existing bill records (default to empty strings if missing).

quotes: I want to add gst number of party whom I sell and their address where they situated
The gst number and address should show in the invoice when print and gst number also show in invoice and summary and print

acceptanceCriteria: Bill records include partyGstNumber and partyAddress fields
addBill and editBill accept partyGstNumber and partyAddress parameters
Existing bill records without these fields default to empty strings and do not cause errors

requirements.text: Add party/buyer input fields to the AddBillForm and EditBillModal components: Party GST Number (text input, auto-filled from the selected party's stored GST if available) and Party Address (text input or textarea, auto-filled from the selected party's stored address if available). These fields should appear in a clearly labeled 'Bill To / Party Details' section without disrupting the Version 24 layout of other fields.
acceptanceCriteria: AddBillForm displays Party GST Number and Party Address input fields
EditBillModal displays Party GST Number and Party Address input fields pre-populated with saved values
When a party is selected via autocomplete, partyGstNumber and partyAddress auto-fill from the party's stored profile
Fields are editable and their values are submitted with the bill

requirements.text: Display the party/buyer's name, GST number, and address in the InvoicePrintView component. These should appear in a 'Bill To' section on the invoice, formatted clearly alongside the existing company/seller details. The GST number must be labeled (e.g., 'GST No:') and the address shown below the party name. This section must be visible both on screen and when printing.

quotes: The gst number and address should show in the invoice when print and gst number also show in invoice and summary and print

acceptanceCriteria: InvoicePrintView shows a 'Bill To' section with party name, GST number (labeled 'GST No:'), and address
The 'Bill To' section is visible on the invoice screen view
The 'Bill To' section is visible and correctly formatted when the invoice is printed or saved as PDF
If partyGstNumber or partyAddress is empty, the section gracefully omits or hides those sub-fields

requirements.text: Display the party/buyer's GST number in the invoice summary views: on the SummaryPage bills table and in the SearchBillsPage results table, add a visible column or inline detail showing the party GST number for each bill. Also ensure the PartySummaryPage shows the party GST number alongside party name.

quotes: gst number also show in invoice and summary and print

acceptanceCriteria: SummaryPage bills table includes party GST number visible per bill row
SearchBillsPage results table shows party GST number per bill row
PartySummaryPage displays party GST number next to each party name

constraints: Do not alter the Version 24 UI layout, color scheme, logo placement, or any existing component design
All immutable frontend paths (hooks, main.tsx, ui components) must not be modified
Backend must remain a single Motoko actor
New bill fields (partyGstNumber, partyAddress) must be backward compatible with existing stored bills
nonGoals: Adding new pages or navigation routes beyond Version 24
Changing company/seller details fields or layout
Implementing a separate party database beyond what already exists
Any redesign or theme change to the existing Version 24 UI
